headline: The greatest kitchens in the world, as seen in HTSI
summary: From soft steel workstations to marvels in majolica, these are the spaces in which we dream of making dinner
body_text:
The open-plan kitchen in Danish gallery owner Tina Seidenfaden Busck’s home in Taarbæk, a municipality 20 minutes north of Copenhagen, was created by combining three small rooms. It’s furnished with brushed-steel worktops, an antique Delft tile splashback and internal windows that allow light to filter through from the sitting room, alongside an artwork by Olafur Eliasson and framed tapestries by Märta Måås-Fjetterström. “Whether it’s to my taste or not, I like when a home tells the story of the people,” says Busck. Ellie Pithers

The kitchen is a vibrant hub for entertaining at the Oxfordshire home of interior designer Peter Mikic and his television-producer husband, Sebastian Scott. A bright-yellow Lacanche oven is surrounded by Paonazzo marble countertops; a long dining table is carved from a London plane tree and surrounded by Pierre Jeanneret-style chairs; and a vast wall of glass brings the indoors into the walled garden, where overflowing borders and structured yew bushes design
